Section 1
Here is the Python function to count word occurrences case-insensitively.
def count_word_occurrences(text, word):
    import re
    text_lower = text.lower()
    word_lower = word.lower()
    pattern = r'\b' + re.escape(word_lower) + r'\b'
    matches = re.findall(pattern, text_lower)
    return len(matches)

Section 2
This solution handles punctuation by using regex word boundaries.
It ensures that partial matches like "freedom's" or "freedom." are treated correctly.
The function converts both inputs to lowercase before comparison.
This guarantees that "Freedom", "FREEDOM", and "freedom" are all counted.

Section 3
Testing the provided example yields the expected result of two.
The text contains "Freedom" twice while ignoring case differences.
Punctuation marks do not interfere with the matching logic here.
The return value is strictly an integer as required.
